methods.description: Genome-wide association study (GWAS) performed using a mixed linear model to identify significant SNPs associated with traits, accounting for population structure and kinship.

steps.description: An established SNP data set (Fu et al., 2013; Li et al., 2012) was used for the GWAS.
steps.searchable_summary: use established SNP data set for GWAS

steps.description: GWAS was performed based on a mixed linear model implemented in TASSEL v3.0 software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform GWAS using mixed linear model in TASSEL v3.0

steps.description: The population structure and kinship were estimated as described by Yu et al. (2006).
The compromised significance threshold for GWAS was determined by Bonferroni correction.
steps.searchable_summary: determine significance threshold using Bonferroni correction

steps.description: The 'n' was determined by PLINK based on SNP pruning (plink -file data -indep-pairwise 50 50 0.1), retaining 46,345 independent SNPs.
steps.searchable_summary: determine number of independent SNPs using PLINK

steps.description: The Manhattan and quantile-quantile plots were generated using the R package qqman.
steps.searchable_summary: generate Manhattan and QQ plots using qqman

steps.description: The phenotypic variation explained by the significant SNP was estimated by an analysis of variance.
